name: shuriken-sdk
title: Metanet social network app protocol
description: The machine-readable source of truth for shuriken-sdk. Every TypeScript type, runtime validator, doc page, llms.txt anchor, and the runtime capabilities() snapshot are generated from this file, so they can never drift from each other or from the live parent handlers.

response.description: parent -> app; one type per method: <method>-response

envelope.signature: verify hex over sha256(JSON.stringify(payload)) against the session pub (V0 wallet.publicKeyHex / V1 identities.app.pub) before resolving

x-shuriken-zk.description: ZK proofs are verified CLIENT-SIDE by the SDK, out of the box: every proofEnvelope received on connection / generate-proof is re-verified locally (the leafHash public signal is recomputed from canonicalId + purpose + pub, then the Groth16 pairing check runs on BN254 via the inlined @noble/curves — no snarkjs). A failed proof rejects the call with ERR_PROOF_INVALID. Manual/server-side: verifyIdentityProof(envelope, canonicalId, pub). Opt-outs (client-side only, never sent on the wire): ConnectParams.verifyProofs, GenerateProofParams.verifyProof (both default true).

vkeys.note: Groth16 verification keys are EMBEDDED in the SDK bundle and SHA-256-pinned to the digests the platform + vault enforce; a pin mismatch throws ERR_VKEY_INTEGRITY and nothing verifies (fail closed).

connection.description: Connect the app to the user's identity. Returns a version-discriminated identity (V0 wallet | V1 purpose-scoped identities | anonymous). Optionally shares purpose identities and mints ZK proofs in the same round trip. RE-CALLABLE — the canonical way to request identities/proofs later, incrementally: already-approved items resolve silently (no overlay), any NEW item re-prompts the user with the full list. Approvals persist across visits; denials are per-visit.
connection.example: const me = await ninja.connect({ request: ['bsv', 'content'], proofs: ['app'] })

request.description: identities to share (V1). Core set today — future platform versions may add more purposes, including custom namespaces; the SDK forwards unknown purposes untouched.

proofs.description: purposes to mint Groth16 proofs for. Core set today — unknown/custom purposes are forwarded untouched.

content.description: pure key purpose — pub + optional proof, NO chain address field

additionalProperties.description: future/custom purposes are passed through verbatim under their own key

pay.description: Ask the user to authorize a payment. Chain is inferred from the params: BSV (multi-recipient; sats->value, usd/fiatValue+currency, fee->reason), ICP (single recipient, named ledger; amount is a WHOLE-token decimal e.g. 1.5, NOT base units), or KDA (single recipient; chain '2' only for now). The SDK never converts amounts — the platform does sats<->fiat conversion. BSV two-phase: the wire response returns a signed-but-UNBROADCAST rawTxHex; the SDK's pay.bsv finalizes it via the Metanet broadcast API when broadcast=true (default, signed with the session genericUseSeed) or returns it authorized-only when broadcast=false.
pay.example: const { txid } = await ninja.pay.bsv([{ address: '1A1z...', sats: 5000, note: 'coffee' }]); await ninja.pay.icp({ token: 'ckUSDC', to, amount: 1.5 }); await ninja.pay.kda({ to, amount: 1.5 })

x-shuriken-sdk-broadcast.auth: x-pubkey/x-signature signed with the connection's genericUseSeed

create-post.description: Publish a post to the Metanet feed on the user's behalf. The app name is forced platform-side from the registered app (anti-spoofing) and cannot be set by the caller.
create-post.example: const { postId } = await ninja.feed.createPost({ headline: 'gm', previewAsset: file })

generate-proof.description: Mint the APP-identity Groth16 zero-knowledge proof binding the session to the user's canonicalId (app-proof-only shortcut; there is no purpose param — the parent only ever mints the app proof here). Proofs for other purposes are requested via connect({ request, proofs }), which is re-callable: approved items resolve silently, new items re-prompt the full list (approvals persist, denials are per-visit). App proofs require a V1 identity.
generate-proof.example: const proof = await ninja.proof.generate({ reason: 'gate premium' })

full-transaction.description: Fetch a full BSV transaction (raw hex + BUMP merkle path) for SPV verification.

token-history.description: Paginated transaction history for a chain.
token-history.example: const { transactions, hasMore } = await ninja.tx.history({ chain: 'bsv', limit: 100 })

geolocation.description: Location access. One-shot resolves the first accurate (<=100m) fix; watch() streams fixes until you stop. Breaking the stream sends geolocation-stop.
geolocation.example: for await (const fix of ninja.geo.watch()) draw(fix)

qr-scan.description: Open the camera QR scanner. Stays open, emitting scan results, until stop() sends qr-scan-stop.
qr-scan.example: const s = ninja.qr.scan(({ rawValue }) => handle(rawValue)); s.stop();

open-link.description: Open an external link, subject to a user consent overlay.

write-clipboard.description: Write text to the user's clipboard. Fire-and-forget: there is no response.

purpose.description: core purposes today: app | bsv | icp | kda | content; future platform versions may add more, including custom namespaces
